title: Intricately Layered Greek Feta Tart
description: A light and flavorful Greek dish featuring puff pastry, charred to perfection and filled with bouncy feta cheese, namul blanched and seasoned with ayurvedic tamasic friendly ingredients, all wrapped in a ti leaf vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a large bowl, combine the crumbled feta cheese, chopped kalamata olives, artichoke hearts, sun dried tomatoes, and chopped fresh parsley.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Spoon the cheese mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Use a knife to cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
Brush the top of the pastry with olive oil and place the tart on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the tart for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
To serve, wrap each slice in a ti leaf and serve immediately.
